When the same conditions were applied to aryl-aryl ketone synthesis, the reaction was fruitless, but switching the ligand from 2,2'-bipyridine to terpy and the additive from ZnBr2 to ZnCl2 (Condition B) gave satisfactory results. The tridentate terpyridine ligand likely stabilizes the more congested diarylnickel intermediate formed during aryl-aryl coupling. For alkyl-alkyl ketone formation, a third condition (Condition C) using Ni(COD)2 and 4,4′-OCH3-bpy with MgCl2 as additive was developed after extensive optimization. The electron-donating methoxy groups on 4,4′-OCH3-bpy may facilitate the more challenging alkyl-alkyl radical cross-coupling by modulating the electron density at nickel.

Title: Photoinduced Nickel-Catalyzed Deaminative Cross-Electrophile Coupling for C(sp2)−C(sp3) and C(sp3)−C(sp3) Bond Formation
Optimum Ligand: 4,4'-dimethoxy-2,2'-bipyridine [4,4'-OCH3-bpy] for arylation of secondary alkyl; 4,4'-di-tert-butyl-2,2'-bipyridine [4,4'-tBu-bpy] for arylation of primary alkyl; 2,6-di(1-pyrazolyl)pyridine [bpp] for alkylation
Reaction: N-Alkylpyridinium salts + aryl/alkyl halides → alkylated arenes or alkanes

Title: Ni-Catalyzed Formal Cross-Electrophile Coupling of Alcohols with Aryl Halides
Optimum Ligand: 4,4′-di-tert-butyl-2,2′-bipyridine [4,4'-tBu-bpy] for secondary alcohols; 3,4,7,8-tetramethyl-1,10-phenanthroline [TMPhen] for primary alcohols
Reaction: Alcohols (via in situ bromination) + aryl halides → alkyl-aryl products

Title: In-Situ Bromination Enables Formal Cross-Electrophile Coupling of Alcohols with Aryl and Alkenyl Halides
Optimum Ligand: 4,4'-di-tert-butyl-N-cyano-(2,2'-bipyridine)-6-carboximidamide [4,4'-tBu-BpyCamCN] + 4,4′-di-tert-butyl-2,2′-bipyridine [4,4'-tBu-bpy] (1:1 mixed-ligand system for general use); 4,4'-di-tert-butyl-N-cyano-(2,2'-bipyridine)-6-carboximidamide [4,'-tBu-BpyCam] alone for electron-deficient aryl bromides, 2-bromopyridines, alkenyl electrophiles
Reaction: Primary and secondary alcohols (via in situ bromination) + aryl/vinyl bromides → C(sp3)-C(sp2) coupled products
Ligand Knowledge: The mixed tBuBpyCam/dtbbpy (1:1) system gave 89% GC yield (79% isolated). Single-ligand controls showed dtbbpy alone gave 34%, tBuBpyCam alone gave 23%, and no ligand gave 2%. tBuBpyCam and dtbbpy exhibit complementary reactivity: dtbbpy-ligated Ni primarily consumes the aryl bromide, whereas tBuBpyCam-ligated Ni favors alkyl bromide consumption and alkyl radical generation. In the dual-catalyst system, (dtbbpy)Ni captures excess alkyl radicals produced by (tBuBpyCam)Ni, yielding a synergistic improvement.

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Electro-Reductive Cross-Coupling of Aliphatic N-Acyl Imides with Alkyl Halides as a Strategy for Dialkyl Ketone Synthesis: Scope and Mechanistic Investigations
Reaction: Alkyl N-acyl imide + alkyl bromide → dialkyl ketone
Ligand Knowledge: Sterically constrained 6,6′-CH3-bpy afforded lower yield compared to both 5,5′-CH3-bpy and 4,4′-dialkylbipyridines, indicating that steric hindrance at the 6,6′ positions disrupts the coordination geometry necessary for the catalytic cycle. The pseudo-Ni(I) pre-catalyst [NiCl(4,4'-tBu-bpy)]2, which features the bulkier 4,4′-tBu-bpy ligand, significantly diminished the reaction yield compared to Ni(OAc)2 with free bpy. Excess bpy ligand (20 mol % vs 10 mol % Ni) slightly lowered the yield from 66% to 46%, suggesting that excess ligand may over-saturate nickel and impede the formation of active monovalent Ni(I) species needed for single-electron oxidation of alkyl bromides.

Title: Effect of 6,6′-Substituents on Bipyridine-Ligated Ni Catalysts for Cross-Electrophile Coupling
Optimum Ligand: 4,4'-di-tert-butyl-6,6'-dimethyl-2,2'-bipyridine (4,4'-tBu-6,6'-CH3-bpy) for high TOF/room temperature XEC; 4,4'-di-tert-butyl-2,2'-bipyridine (4,4'-tBu-bpy) for conventional XEC with free alkyl radicals
Reaction: Aryl bromides + alkyl bromides → alkylated arenes (Ni/Co dual catalytic); aryl iodides + alkyl Katritzky salts → alkylated arenes (reductive)
Ligand Knowledge: Mechanistically, bulkier 6,6′-substituents hinder coordination to Ni(COD)2, slow oxidative addition, and produce high-spin unstable (4,4'-tBu-6,6'-CH3-bpy)Ni(Ar)I intermediates that trap primary and benzylic alkyl radicals more slowly than low-spin (4,4'-tBu-bpy)Ni(Ar)I.

Title: Ni-Catalyzed Deoxygenative Cross-Coupling of Alcohols with Aryl Chlorides via an Organic Photoredox Process
Reaction: Alcohols (via benzoxazolium salt adducts) + aryl chlorides → deoxygenative arylation products (C(sp2)–C(sp3))

Title: Photoredox/Nickel Dual Catalysis for C(sp2)−C(sp3) Cross-Electrophile Coupling Reaction of Mesylates of Phenols and Primary Alcohols
Reaction: Aryl mesylate + primary alkyl mesylate → C(sp2)–C(sp3) coupled product
Ligand Knowledge: A mixed nitrogen/phosphorus ligand system was essential for this transformation. Using dtbbpy (NL1) alone gave 62% yield of product 3, with 34% of reduced byproduct naphthalene 4, as the NL1-ligated Ni-catalyst fully consumed aryl mesylate 1 within 24 h but could not suppress over-reduction. Using PCy3 (PL1) alone gave only 11% yield, with sluggish 70% conversion of 1 and 55% of naphthalene 4 as the major side product. The mixed-ligand system synergistically balanced oxidative addition of the aryl mesylate by the nitrogen ligand with alkyl radical capture by the phosphine ligand, selectively producing the cross-coupled product over reduced byproduct.

Title: Ni/Ti Dual Catalyzed Cross-Electrophile Coupling between Unactivated Alkyl Chlorides and Aryl Halides
Reaction: Alkyl chloride + aryl bromide → aryl-alkyl cross-coupled product
Ligand Knowledge: The key role of 4,4'-tBu-bpy is to facilitate oxidative addition of the aryl halide to form the (4,4'-tBu-bpy)Ni(Ar)X intermediate, which then captures Ti-generated alkyl radicals for C–C bond formation.

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Decarboxylative Alkylation of Aryl Iodides with Anhydrides
Reaction: Aliphatic carboxylic anhydrides + aryl iodides → alkylated arenes

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Reductive Electrophilic Ring Opening of Cycloketone Oxime Esters with Aroyl Chlorides
Reaction: cycloketone oxime esters (alkyl radical donor via C-C bond cleavage) + aroyl chlorides (acyl donor) → cyanoketones
Ligand Knowledge: Only phenanthroline-type ligands were effective; the rigid planar scaffold likely stabilizes the key Ni intermediate.

Title: Migratory Reductive Acylation between Alkyl Halides or Alkenes and Alkyl Carboxylic Acids by Nickel Catalysis
Reaction: Alkyl bromides + carboxylic acids → benzylic ketones (via migratory reductive acylation)
Ligand Knowledge: The C6-methyl substituted bipyridine ligand with NiBr2·3H2O gave the migratory acylation product in 70% isolated yield as essentially a single regioisomer. Critically, replacement of 6,6'-CH3-bpy by the parent bipyridine ligand (without C6-methyl substituents) resulted in only the ipso-coupling product with no migratory product observed, demonstrating the essential role of the C6-alkyl substituents in the ligand backbone. When Ni(cod)2 was used as precatalyst, only ipso-coupling was observed even with L1 present. The C6-methyl group on the bipyridine backbone is proposed to enable the NiH chainwalking mechanism by modulating the coordination geometry and stability of nickel hydride intermediates, thereby favoring migratory insertion over direct reductive elimination at the ipso position.

Title: Enantioselective Electroreductive Coupling of Alkenyl and Benzyl Halides via Nickel Catalysis
Reaction: alkenyl bromides (C(sp2) electrophile) + benzyl chlorides (C(sp3) electrophile) → enantioenriched alkenes bearing allylic stereogenic centers
Ligand Knowledge: The indanyl-substituted bis(oxazoline) is essential for enantioinduction in the electrochemical version.

Title: Nickel/Cobalt-Catalyzed C(sp3)−C(sp3) Cross-Coupling of Alkyl Halides with Alkyl Tosylates
Reaction: Alkyl bromide + alkyl tosylate → C(sp3)–C(sp3) coupled product
Ligand Knowledge: The bipyridine ligand structure had a dramatic impact on the Ni/Co-catalyzed C(sp3)-C(sp3) cross-coupling. Unsubstituted 2,2'-bipyridine (bipy) gave 70% yield. Substituents at the 6,6′-positions completely suppressed the coupling, with 6,6'-CH3-bpy giving only trace yield due to steric blockage of the coordination site. Electron-withdrawing 4,4'-CO2Me-bpy gave only 8% and electron-donating 4,4'-OCH3-bpy gave 31%. As the bulkiness of the 4,4'-substituents increased, the yield trended upward: 4,4'-Ph-bpy gave 48%, 4,4'-Mes-bpy gave 77%, and 4,4'-tBu-bpy gave the highest yield at 83% (77% isolated). The increasing steric bulk at the 4,4'-positions favors cross-coupling over homocoupling, likely by modulating the reductive elimination step and preventing radical dimerization pathways. The reaction without NiCl2(bpy) or without VB12 gave 0% yield, confirming both catalyst components are essential.

Title: Expanding the Medicinal Chemist Toolbox: Comparing Seven C(sp2)-C(sp3) Cross-Coupling Methods by Library Synthesis
Reaction: Aryl bromides + alkyl bromides → alkylated arenes (via reductive CEC among seven methods compared)
Ligand Knowledge: This is a methods comparison study rather than a ligand screening paper. The reductive CEC method used NiCl2(glyme) (7 mol%) with 4,4′-di-tert-butyl-2,2′-bipyridine as the ligand (7 mol%) in DMA with Zn and TFA at 60 °C. The reductive CEC with dtbbpy delivered moderate-to-good yields with primary and secondary alkyl bromides and was comparable to the photoredox CEC method using Ir[dF(CF3)ppy]2(dtbbpy)PF6/NiCl2(dtbbpy). Neither CEC method could install tertiary or benzylic groups.

Title: Replacing Conventional Carbon Nucleophiles with Electrophiles: Nickel-Catalyzed Reductive Alkylation of Aryl Bromides and Chlorides
Reaction: Aryl/vinyl bromides (and activated aryl chlorides) + alkyl bromides → alkylated arenes
Ligand Knowledge: The synergism between bipyridine and bisphosphine ligands observed for aryl iodide coupling was not reproduced for bromide couplings: ligands 4,4′-di-tert-butyl-2,2′-bipyridine + 1,2-bis(diphenylphosphino)benzene with Mn gave only 39% yield and 17% aryl homodimer. The best results were obtained using bipyridine ligands alone. 4,4'-dimethoxy-2,2'-bipyridine gave 77% yield with minimal byproducts (1% aryl dimer, 1% alkyl dimer). 4,4'-di-tert-butyl-2,2'-bipyridine gave 67% yield, and 1,10-phenanthroline gave 73% yield. For electron-poor aryl bromides,1,10-phenanthroline provided better results than 4,4'-dimethoxy-2,2'-bipyridine.

Title: Catalytic Asymmetric Reductive Acyl Cross-Coupling: Synthesis of Enantioenriched Acyclic alpha,alpha-Disubstituted Ketones
Reaction: Acid chlorides + racemic secondary benzyl chlorides → α,α-disubstituted ketones
Ligand Knowledge: The isopropylidene bridge of R,R-Ph-BOX(c-C3H4) proved critical, as R,R-Ph-BOXCH2 afforded only 10% yield and 0% ee (no stereoinduction). Tridentate ligand PyBOX led to almost exclusive homocoupling. R,R-Ph-BOX(c-C3H4) diphenyl substituents and isopropylidene bridge create the appropriate chiral environment around Ni to control the stereoconvergent oxidative addition of the racemic benzyl chloride, enabling high enantioselectivity in the reductive elimination step.

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Asymmetric Reductive Cross-Coupling Between Vinyl and Benzyl Electrophiles
Reaction: vinyl bromides (C(sp2) electrophile) + benzyl chlorides (C(sp3) electrophile) → alkenes bearing aryl-substituted tertiary allylic stereogenic centers
Ligand Knowledge: Tuning the central linker from isopropylidene to cyclopropyl optimizes the bite angle for both yield and enantioselectivity.

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Reductive Cross-Coupling of Aryl Halides with Alkyl Halides
Reaction: Aryl iodides + alkyl iodides → alkylated arenes

Title: Dual Catalytic Strategy Forging sp2-sp3 and sp3-sp3 Architectures via beta-Scission of Aliphatic Alcohol Derivatives
Reaction: N-phthalimide ethers (aliphatic alcohol derivatives) + aryl halides → sp3-arylated products; N-phthalimide ethers + alkyl halides → sp3-alkylated products
Ligand Knowledge: For C(sp2)-C(sp3) coupling, 4,4'-tBu-bpy was particularly suited for the targeted C–C bond-forming event. For C(sp3)-C(sp3) coupling, the Ni/4,4'-tBu-bpy regime completely failed to provide even traces of product, whereas switching to the Ni/tBu-terpy couple (a tridentate ligand) successfully delivered C(sp3)-C(sp3) coupling frameworks, revealing a critical impact of ligand denticity. The bidentate ligand favors recombination of the aryl-Ni(II) intermediate with the open-shell species generated from beta-scission, but the electronic differences between aryl- and alkyl-Ni(II) complexes make 4,4'-tBu-bpy unsuitable for C(sp3)-C(sp3) coupling. The tridentate ligand overcomes this limitation, presumably by altering the coordination geometry and electronics to favor radical capture with alkyl-Ni(II) species.

Title: Diversifying Amino Acids and Peptides via Deaminative Reductive Cross-Couplings Leveraging High-Throughput Experimentation
Optimum Ligand: 2,2′-bipyridine [bipy] with TDAE for most aryl bromides; pyridine-2,6-bis(carboximidamide) [PyBCam] with Mn or Zn for acidic aryl bromides and peptide substrates
Reaction: Amino acid/peptide pyridinium salts + aryl bromides → noncanonical amino acids and peptide derivatives

Title: Formation of C(sp2)−C(sp3) Bonds Instead of Amide C−N Bonds from Carboxylic Acid and Amine Substrate Pools by Decarbonylative Cross-Electrophile Coupling
Optimum Ligand: 2,2'-bipyridine [bipy] for standard substrates; 4,4'-dimethoxy-2,2'-bipyridine [4,4'-OCH3-bpy] for thalidomide derivatives
Reaction: 2-pyridyl aryl carboxylic acid esters + N-alkyl pyridinium salts/alkyl bromides or iodides → decarbonylative C(sp2)–C(sp3) cross-coupling products
Ligand Knowledge: Ligand identity had a profound effect on selectivity between decarbonylation and ketone formation. Bipyridine ligands both provided primarily the decarbonylation cross-product. In contrast, tBu-terpy switched selectivity almost entirely to ketone formation. The pyridone ligand generated in situ from 2-pyridyl ester oxidative addition was shown by X-ray crystallography to be N-bound to Ni and accelerates decarbonylation compared to bromide ligands, enabling the reversible oxidative addition/decarbonylation sequence essential for cross-product formation.

Title: Formal Cross-Coupling of Amines and Carboxylic Acids to Form sp3-sp2 Carbon-Carbon Bonds
Optimum Ligand: 5,5'-dimethyl-2,2'-bipyridine [5,5'-CH3-bpy] for primary alkylpyridinium salts; 5,5'-bis(trifluoromethyl)-2,2'-bipyridine [5,5'-CF3-bpy] for secondary/benzyl alkylpyridinium salts
Reaction: N-acyl-glutarimide (from aryl carboxylic acid) + primary/secondary alkylpyridinium salt (from amine) → decarbonylated C(sp3)-C(sp2) product

Title: Enantioselective C(sp3)-C(sp3) Reductive Cross-Electrophile Coupling of Unactivated Alkyl Halides with alpha-Chloroboronates via Dual Nickel/Photoredox Catalysis
Reaction: racemic alpha-chloroboronates + unactivated alkyl iodides → chiral secondary alkyl boronic esters
Ligand Knowledge: The proposed catalytic cycle involves oxidative addition of the alpha-chloroboronate to (L)NiI, reduction to (L)NiII, then capture of the alkyl radical to form a Ni(III) intermediate, followed by enantioselective reductive elimination. The ligand is essential for both stereocontrol and enabling the Ni(I) catalytic cycle.

Title: Computational Methods Enable the Prediction of Improved Catalysts for Nickel-Catalyzed Cross-Electrophile Coupling
Reaction: aryl halides + alkyl halides → C(sp2)-C(sp3) cross-coupled products; aryl halides + N-alkylpyridinium salts → arylated pyridinium products; N-hydroxyphthalimide esters + aryl bromides → decarboxylative arylation products; aryl chlorides + alkyl chlorides → cross-coupled products
Ligand Knowledge: 4,4'-NMe2-bpy (R = CH3) provides a strongly donating ligand that stabilizes the square planar (L)NiII(Ar)Br intermediate and decreases the NPA charge of the phenyl C-ipso, leading to a 5-fold increase in selectivity over 4,4'-tBu-bpy.

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Enantioselective C(sp3)-C(sp3) Cross-Electrophile Coupling of N-Sulfonyl Styrenyl Aziridines with Alkyl Bromides
Reaction: N-sulfonyl styrenyl aziridines + primary alkyl bromides → phenethylamine derivatives

Title: Non-Innocent Role of Sacrificial Anodes in Electrochemical Nickel-Catalyzed C(sp2)-C(sp3) Cross-Electrophile Coupling
Reaction: piperidine-derived alkyl bromide + indole-derived aryl bromide → C(sp2)-C(sp3) cross-coupled pharmaceutical intermediate; various p-substituted aryl bromides + alkyl bromide → cross-coupled products

Title: Cross-Electrophile Coupling to Form Sterically Hindered C(sp2)-C(sp3) Bonds: Ni and Co Afford Complementary Reactivity
Optimum Ligand: 4,4'-di-tert-butyl-2,2'-bipyridine [4,4-tBu-bpy] for Ni system; 2,2'-bipyridine [bipy] for Co system
Reaction: secondary alkyl bromides + ortho-substituted aryl bromides → sterically hindered C(sp2)-C(sp3) coupled products; secondary alkyl bromides + 2,6-disubstituted aryl iodides → sterically hindered C(sp2)-C(sp3) coupled products (via Co catalysis)
Ligand Knowledge: Electron-neutral to electron-rich bipyridine ligands performed best. The bipyridine ligands are essential for both Ni and Co oxidative addition and radical capture steps with hindered arenes.

Title: Enantioselective Radical-Radical Cross-Couplings of beta-Hydroxy Amides and N-Hydroxyphthalimide Esters via Ni/Photoredox Catalysis
Reaction: primary alkyl NHP esters (from carboxylic acids) + secondary beta-hydroxy amides (from alkyl alcohols) → enantioenriched C(sp3)-C(sp3) coupled products with acyclic tertiary stereocenters
Ligand Knowledge: Mechanistic studies revealed that the chiral diamine-ligated Ni catalyst operates via a Ni(I)/Ni(II) cycle: the primary alkyl radical is captured by (L)NiIBr to form a persistent metal-alkyl complex (the resting state), which then intercepts the secondary alkyl radical. The radical combination step is rate-determining (secondary KIE of 0.84). The ligand's ability to preferentially stabilize the less substituted primary alkyl radical as a persistent Ni-alkyl species enables both cross-selectivity and enantiocontrol.

Title: Water as an Electron Donor for Cross-Electrophile Coupling Reactions
Optimum Ligand: 4,4′-di-tert-butyl-2,2′-bipyridine [4,4'-tBu-bpy]; Bathocuproine [BCP] for electron-rich arenes and C(sp2)-C(sp2) couplings; 2,6-di(1-pyrazolyl)pyridine [bpp] for secondary alkyl-alkyl C(sp3)-C(sp3) couplings
Reaction: aryl bromides + primary/secondary alkyl bromides → aryl-alkyl cross-coupled products; primary alkyl bromides + primary alkyl bromides → Csp3-Csp3 coupled products; aryl bromides + aryl bromides → biaryl products
Ligand Knowledge: dtbbpy was optimal for the primary aryl + alkyl bromide coupling (87% yield). For electron-rich arenes, Bathocuproine proved superior to dtbbpy. For C(sp3)-C(sp3) couplings involving secondary bromides, 2,6-di(1-pyrazolyl)pyridine was required to promote coupling. C(sp2)-C(sp2) biaryl coupling (73% yield) also required Bathocuproine.

Title: Decarboxylative Cross-Electrophile Coupling of N-Hydroxyphthalimide Esters with Aryl Iodides
Reaction: N-hydroxyphthalimide (NHP) esters + aryl iodides → alkylated arenes

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Defluorinative Reductive Cross-Coupling of gem-Difluoroalkenes with Unactivated Secondary and Tertiary Alkyl Halides
Reaction: gem-difluoroalkenes + secondary/tertiary alkyl halides (bromides and iodides) → monofluoroalkenes
Ligand Knowledge: The electron-donating methoxy groups at the 4,7-positions are critical for stabilizing the Ni center and facilitating the challenging defluorinative cross-coupling, particularly with sterically hindered tertiary alkyl halides.

Title: Remote Migratory Cross-Electrophile Coupling and Olefin Hydroarylation Reactions Enabled by in Situ Generation of NiH
Reaction: alkyl bromides + aryl bromides → 1,1-diarylalkanes (migratory cross-electrophile coupling); alkenes + aryl bromides → hydroarylation products (migratory olefin hydroarylation)
Ligand Knowledge: The 6,6'-methyl groups are critical for enabling the migratory coupling pathway — use of parent 2,2'-bipyridine or 6,6'-OCH3-bpy resulted in only the linear (non-migratory) product. 6,6'-CH3-bipyridine promotes selective oxidative addition of Ni(0) to the unactivated alkyl bromide (rather than the aryl bromide), followed by beta-hydrogen elimination to generate a NiH species that undergoes iterative chain-walking to the benzylic position.

Title: Ni-Catalyzed Reductive Coupling of Electron-Rich Aryl Iodides with Tertiary Alkyl Halides
Reaction: electron-rich aryl iodides + tertiary alkyl bromides → arylated quaternary carbon centers

Title: Metallaphotoredox-Catalyzed Cross-Electrophile Csp3-Csp3 Coupling of Aliphatic Bromides
Optimum Ligand: 4,4′-di-tert-butyl-2,2′-bipyridine [4,4'-tBu-bpy] for general couplings; 2,6-bis(4,5-dihydrooxazol-2-yl)pyridine [PyBox] for secondary-secondary couplings
Reaction: aliphatic bromides (piperidinyl, THP, cyclohexyl) + methyl tosylate/small alkyl halides → Csp3-Csp3 coupled products; secondary aliphatic bromides + secondary aliphatic bromides → secondary-secondary coupled products
Ligand Knowledge: For the long-standing challenge of coupling two secondary aliphatic centers, the tridentate PyBox ligand is essential because it successfully prevents metal-alkyl bond isomerization that plagues Pd- and Ni-catalyzed cross-couplings.

Title: A Radical Approach to Anionic Chemistry: Synthesis of Ketones, Alcohols, and Amines
Reaction: redox-active esters (RAEs, from carboxylic acids) + carboxylic acids (activated in situ as anhydrides) → unsymmetrical ketones; RAEs + chiral sulfinimines → amines

Title: Reductive Coupling between C-N and C-O Electrophiles
Reaction: benzyl/aryl ammonium salts (C-N electrophile, radical donor) + vinyl/aryl C-O electrophiles (vinyl acetates, aryl triflates/tosylates) → allylic arenes, diarylmethanes
Ligand Knowledge: The phenanthroline ligand likely facilitates single-electron processes at nickel to generate alkyl radicals from ammonium salts.

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Electrochemical Couplings of Vinyl Halides: Synthetic and Stereochemical Aspects
Reaction: vinyl halides + aryl halides or activated olefins → conjugated dienes, unsaturated esters, ketones, nitriles

Title: Selective Cross-Coupling of Organic Halides with Allylic Acetates
Optimum Ligand: 4,4',4''-tri-tert-butyl-2,2':6',2''-terpyridine [tBu-terpy] for standard aryl iodide + allylic acetate coupling; Neocuproine [DMPhen] for vinyl bromide + branched allylic acetate coupling
Reaction: aryl iodides/bromides (electron-rich, electron-poor, functionalized) + substituted allylic acetates (primary, secondary, cyclic, vinyl, 1,3-disubstituted) → allylarenes; also vinyl bromides + branched allylic acetates → skipped-diene products
Ligand Knowledge: The tridentate ligand suppresses aryl halide dimerization and biaryl formation because (L)Ni complexes dimerize aryl halides only slowly while remaining reactive toward allylic acetates, unlike bidentate ligands. For vinyl halide couplings with branched allylic acetate, the increased steric hindrance of DMPhen suppresses allyl dimerization.

Title: Reductive Cross-Coupling of Nonaromatic, Heterocyclic Bromides with Aryl and Heteroaryl Bromides
Reaction: nonaromatic heterocyclic bromides (piperidine, tetrahydropyran, tetrahydrofuran, oxetane, pyrrolidine, azetidine) + aryl and heteroaryl bromides → heterocycle-aryl cross-coupled products

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Direct Electrochemical Cross-Coupling between Aryl Halides and Activated Alkyl Halides
Reaction: aryl halides (aryl iodides and bromides) + activated alkyl halides (α-chloroketones, α-chloroesters, allylic acetates) → α-arylketones, α-arylesters, and allylated arenes
Ligand Knowledge: NiBr2(bipy) outperformed phosphine-based ligands NiBr2(PPh3)2 and NiBr2(diphos), which both failed entirely—their Ni(0) species reacted only with the activated alkyl halide (RX) and not with ArX, because the oxidative addition of ArX to Ni(0) was too slow. NiCl2(phen) gave comparable yields to NiBr2(bipy) (73% vs 80% in one example), indicating phenanthroline and bipyridine perform similarly. Adding excess bipy to NiBr2(bipy) (i.e., NiBr2(bipy) + bipy) significantly reduced yield from 80% to 30%, because excess bipyridine slows the rate-determining oxidative addition of ArX to Ni(0), favoring the competing pathway of RX oxidative addition and homocoupling. Ni(BF4)2(bipy) failed to consume ArX.

Title: Asymmetric Induction in the Electrochemical Cross-Coupling of Aryl Halides with α-Chloropropionic Acid Derivatives Catalyzed by Nickel Complexes
Reaction: aryl halides (iodobenzene, m-bromo-α,α,α-trifluorotoluene) + α-chloropropionic acid derivatives bearing chiral auxiliaries → α-arylpropionic acid derivatives (e.g., naproxen, flurbiprofen precursors) with diastereoselective induction

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Reductive Cross-Coupling of Unactivated Alkyl Halides
Optimum Ligand: 2-((S)-4-((R)-sec-butyl)-4,5-dihydrooxazol-2-yl)-6-((S)-4-((S)-sec-butyl)-4,5-dihydrooxazol-2-yl)pyridine [PyBoxsBu] for coupling with primary alkyl iodides; 2,2'-(4-chloropyridine-2,6-diyl)bis(4,5-dihydrooxazole) [4-Cl-PyBox] for coupling with primary alkyl bromides
Reaction: secondary alkyl halides (e.g., 4-bromo-1-tosylpiperidine) + primary alkyl iodides/bromides → C(sp3)-C(sp3) cross-coupled products
Ligand Knowledge: Tuning the PyBox ligand structure was crucial to suppress competitive homocoupling.

Title: Synthesis of Functionalized Dialkyl Ketones from Carboxylic Acid Derivatives and Alkyl Halides
Reaction: carboxylic acid chlorides + alkyl iodides → unsymmetrical dialkyl ketones; (2-pyridyl)thioesters + alkyl iodides → unsymmetrical dialkyl ketones; acid chlorides + benzylic chlorides → dialkyl ketones
Ligand Knowledge: 4.4'-tBu-bpy was critical for both reactivity and selectivity. Without ligand, only 8% ketone was formed versus 89% with 4,4'-tBu-bpy. In contrast, tBu-terpy produced only 27% ketone yield with 54% alkyl dimer as the major side product, demonstrating that 4,4'-tBu-bpy effectively suppresses dimerization of the alkyl iodide partner while maintaining high cross-coupling efficiency.

Title: Ketone Formation via Mild Nickel-Catalyzed Reductive Coupling of Alkyl Halides with Aryl Acid Chlorides
Reaction: unactivated alkyl iodides (primary and secondary, cyclic and acyclic) + aryl/alkyl acid chlorides → alkyl aryl ketones; sterically hindered secondary bromides + acid chlorides → ketones (using ligand 4b)

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Reductive Coupling of Aryl Halides with Secondary Alkyl Bromides and Allylic Acetate
Reaction: aryl bromides (electron-rich, electron-poor, heteroaryl) + secondary alkyl bromides (cyclic, open-chain) → C(sp2)–C(sp3) alkyl–aryl products; also activated aryl chlorides + cyclohexyl bromide → alkyl–aryl products; aryl bromides + allylic acetate → allyl–aryl products
Ligand Knowledge: The combination of 4,4'-tBu-bpy with 1 equiv pyridine and lowered temperature (25 °C) dramatically improved yield to 92%, whereas 10% pyridine gave only 46%, indicating that the bidentate 4,4'-tBu-bpy + pyridine as a co-ligand is critical for suppressing homocoupling of the alkyl partner.

Title: Reductive Cross-Coupling of 3-Bromo-2,1-borazaronaphthalenes with Alkyl Iodides
Reaction: 3-bromo-2,1-borazaronaphthalenes + primary and secondary alkyl iodides → alkylated azaborines
Ligand Knowledge: Among several bipyridine ligands tested during optimization, 4,4'-CH3-bpy gave the highest conversion to the desired cross-coupled product, with the major side product being protodehalogenation of the azaborine core.

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Reductive Methylation of Alkyl Halides and Acid Chlorides with Methyl p-Tosylate
Optimum Ligand: 4,4′-di-tert-butyl-2,2′-bipyridine [4,4'-tBu-bpy] for alkyl halide methylation; 5,5'-dimethyl-2,2'-bipyridine [5,5'-CH3-bpy] for ketone synthesis
Reaction: alkyl bromides + methyl p-tosylate → methylated alkanes; aryl acid chlorides + methyl p-tosylate → methyl ketones

Title: Nickel-catalyzed reductive coupling of homoenolates and their higher homologues with unactivated alkyl bromides
Reaction: cyclic anhydrides + unactivated alkyl bromides → beta- or gamma-alkylated aliphatic acids (via decarbonylative cross-coupling)

Title: Metallaphotoredox deuteroalkylation utilizing thianthrenium salts
Reaction: d2-alkyl thianthrenium salts + aryl bromides → deuterated alkylarenes; d2-alkyl thianthrenium salts + alkyl bromides → deuterated alkanes

Title: LMCT-driven electron relay unlocks alcohols as tunable reductants for nickel-catalyzed cross-electrophilic couplings
Optimum Ligand: 4,4'-diphenyl-2,2'-bipyridine [4,4'-Ph-bpy] for standard XEC; chiral bioxazoline for asymmetric variants
Reaction: aryl bromides + alkyl bromides → aryl-alkyl cross-coupled products; alpha-chloroboronates + aryl bromides → chiral secondary alkyl boronic esters (asymmetric variant)

Title: Persistent organonickel complexes as general platforms for Csp2–Csp3 coupling reactions
Reaction: aryl/vinyl/heteroaryl electrophiles (halides, OTf) + alkyl electrophiles (halides, RAEs, pyridiniums, perfluoroalkyl halides) → alkyl-aryl/alkyl-vinyl C(sp2)–C(sp3) coupled products
Ligand Knowledge: MeBPI uniquely enabled near-quantitative formation of persistent NiIIAr complexes, whereas tridentate terpy and PyBCamCN failed to reach full conversion (mostly Ar–H), and bidentate 4,4'-tBu-bpy produced exclusively biaryl homocoupling (100% Ar–Ar, 0% NiIIAr). MeBPI's built-in anionic donor and phthalimide-like X-type motif stabilize the NiIIAr intermediate against decomposition, enabling coupling of previously inaccessible substrate combinations (aryl chlorides with tertiary alkyl pyridiniums, RAEs, etc.).

Title: Reductive alkyl–alkyl coupling from isolable nickel–alkyl complexes
Reaction: alkyl halides + alkyl RAEs/pyridinium salts/alkyl halides → C(sp3)–C(sp3) coupled products
Ligand Knowledge: The MeBPI pincer ligand enables isolation of unusually persistent Ni(alkyl) complexes that serve as synthetic intermediates for C(sp3)-C(sp3) cross-coupling. Crystallographic analysis revealed that the τ4 geometric distortion parameter is critical: complexes with τ4 <= 0.3 gave the highest coupling yields, while the more distorted 2-Cy analogue provide low yield. The rigid tridentate MeBPI scaffold enforces a square planar geometry that prevents β-hydride elimination and stabilizes simple alkyl fragments, a capability not achieved with bis(phosphine) or salicylaldiminate ligands.

Title: Mild ketone formation via Ni-catalyzed reductive coupling of unactivated alkyl halides with acid anhydrides
Optimum Ligand: Bathophenanthroline [BPhen], for alkyl iodides; 4,4'-dimethyl-2,2'-bipyridine [4,4'-CH3-bpy] for alkyl bromides
Reaction: unactivated alkyl iodide + aryl acid anhydride → ketones; unactivated alkyl bromide + aryl acid anhydride → ketones

Title: Nickel-catalyzed cross-coupling of unactivated alkyl halides using bis(pinacolato)diboron as reductant
Reaction: secondary alkyl bromide + primary alkyl bromide → C(sp3)–C(sp3) cross-coupling product
Ligand Knowledge: PyrIm in combination with LiOMe base, promotes formation of Ni–Bpin catalytic intermediates that are key to differentiating secondary and primary alkyl bromides, enabling chemoselective cross-coupling.

Title: Dual nickel and Lewis acid catalysis for cross-electrophile coupling: the allylation of aryl halides with allylic alcohols
Reaction: aryl halides + allylic alcohols → linear (E)-allylarenes

Title: Alkenylation of unactivated alkyl bromides through visible light photocatalysis
Reaction: unactivated alkyl bromides + vinyl phenyl sulfones → alkenes (method A, photoredox only); unactivated alkyl bromides + vinyl bromides → alkenes (method B, dual photoredox/Ni)

Title: Ni-catalyzed migratory fluoro-alkenylation of unactivated alkyl bromides with gem-difluoroalkenes
Reaction: unactivated alkyl bromides + gem-difluoroalkenes → monofluoroalkenes (via migratory chain-walking and defluorinative coupling)
Ligand Knowledge: Increasing steric bulk at the ortho-position completely shut down reactivity, while removing the ortho-substituents entirely also gave no product, demonstrating the essential role of the 6,6'-methyl groups. The structural analogue 2,9-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline showed comparable catalytic efficiency, but bathocuproine was less effective.

Title: Nickel-catalyzed monofluoromethylation of (hetero)aryl bromides via reductive cross-coupling
Reaction: (hetero)aryl bromides + BrCH2F → ArCH2F (monofluoromethylated arenes)

Title: Nickel-catalyzed C–N bond activation: activated primary amines as alkylating reagents in reductive cross-coupling
Optimum Ligand: 2,2'-bipyridine [bipy] for secondary alkyl pyridinium salts; 4,4',4''-tri-tert-butyl-2,2':6',2''-terpyridine [tBu-terpy] for primary alkyl pyridinium salts
Reaction: Katritzky pyridinium salts (from activated primary/secondary amines) + aryl iodides/bromides → alkylated arenes
Ligand Knowledge: DFT calculations confirmed 2,2'-bipyridine facilitates oxidative addition of the aryl halide to Ni(0) followed by SET reduction of the pyridinium salt to generate the alkyl radical.

Title: Ni-catalyzed cross-electrophile coupling between vinyl/aryl and alkyl sulfonates: synthesis of cycloalkenes and modification of peptides
Reaction: vinyl triflates + alkyl mesylates/tosylates → functionalized cycloalkenes; aryl triflates + alkyl mesylates → aryl-alkyl coupled products; vinyl triflates + tyrosine sulfonates → peptide-modified alkenes
Ligand Knowledge: Terpy's tridentate coordination is essential for controlling the highly mismatched reactivity between vinyl triflates (highly reactive) and unactivated alkyl mesylates (poorly reactive), selectively directing oxidative addition to the vinyl electrophile while enabling radical activation of the alkyl partner via in situ mesylate-to-iodide exchange.

Title: Preparation of alpha-amino acids via Ni-catalyzed reductive vinylation and arylation of alpha-pivaloyloxy glycine
Reaction: N-carbonyl-protected α-pivaloyloxy glycine derivatives (glycinyl radical donor) + vinyl/aryl halides and triflates (C(sp2) electrophile) → α-vinyl/aryl amino acids

Title: Synthesis of chiral branched allylamines through dual photoredox/nickel catalysis
Reaction: alkyl bromides + 3-bromoallylamines → branched allylamines
Ligand Knowledge: The dtbbpy/NiCl2 conditions were adopted from the authors' prior work on allyl alcohol coupling; with 4CzIPN as the photocatalyst, the model reaction gave 82% yield (85% with an Ir photocatalyst). No other ligands were screened. An Ir/NHC/TMEDA system from Yu et al. failed completely for coupling with t-BuBr, whereas dtbbpy enabled broad substrate scope while preserving enantiomeric purity of chiral N-protected allylamine products.

Title: Nickel-catalyzed cross-electrophile coupling of aryl chlorides with allylic alcohols
Reaction: aryl chlorides + allylic alcohols → allylarenes (linear, E-configured)

Title: Nickel-catalyzed electrochemical reductive relay cross-coupling of alkyl halides with alkyl carboxylic acids
Reaction: alkyl carboxylic acids + alkyl bromides → dialkyl ketones
Ligand Knowledge: 6,6'-CH3-bpy was optimal, affording the relay cross-coupling product in 77% NMR yield (72% isolated). Replacing ligand 1 with ligand 2 decreased the yield to 42%, and omission of both Ni and ligand gave no product. Cyclic voltammetry showed that 6,6'-CH3-bpy facilitates electrochemical reduction of Ni(II) to Ni(0)/Ni(I), enabling the catalytic cycle at room temperature with good functional group tolerance (yields up to 78%).

Title: Nickel-catalyzed asymmetric reductive cross-coupling of alpha-chloroesters with (hetero)aryl iodides
Reaction: alpha-chloroesters + (hetero)aryl iodides → alpha-aryl esters
Ligand Knowledge: Enantioselectivity improved with beta-branched alpha-chloroesters. Multivariate linear regression modeling revealed that steric matching between the ligand's minimum width (B1) and the substrate's length (L) governs enantioselectivity.

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Cross-Electrophile Allylation of Vinyl Bromides and the Modification of Anti-Tumour Natural Medicine beta-Elemene
Reaction: allylic acetates (allyl electrophile) + (E)-alkenyl bromides (vinyl electrophile) → 1,4-dienes

Title: Nickel-catalyzed reductive coupling of arylcarboxylic acid 2-pyridyl esters with alkyl methanesulfonates: access to alkyl aryl ketones
Reaction: arylcarboxylic acid 2-pyridyl esters + alkyl methanesulfonates → alkyl aryl ketones

Title: Carboxylic acid-assisted sterically demanding reductive cross-coupling between cycloalkenyl and alkyl bromides
Reaction: 2-bromo cycloalkenyl carboxylic acids + alkyl bromides → all-carbon tetrasubstituted cycloalkenes
Ligand Knowledge: The simpler bipyridine scaffold proved most effective for this sterically demanding coupling, where the carboxylic acid group assists via κ2-coordination to Ni, and the ligand must accommodate this directing interaction without adding steric bulk.

Title: Nickel-catalyzed C(sp2)-C(sp3) coupling via photoactive electron donor-acceptor complexes
Reaction: aryl bromides + alkyl iodides → alkylated arenes

Title: Nickel-catalyzed reductive cross-coupling of monofluoroalkyl triflates with aryl halides for monofluoroalkylated arenes
Reaction: monofluoroalkyl triflates + aryl halides → monofluoroalkylated arenes

Title: Nickel-catalyzed reductive coupling of 2-pyridyl esters with unactivated alkyl chlorides: a universal synthesis of aryl-alkyl and dialkyl ketones via dynamic halide exchange
Reaction: 2-pyridyl esters + unactivated alkyl chlorides → aryl-alkyl and dialkyl ketones

Title: Gram-Scale Ketone Synthesis by Direct Reductive Coupling of Alkyl Iodides with Acid Chlorides
Reaction: alkyl iodides + aroyl chlorides → alkyl aryl ketones

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Cross-Electrophile Coupling of 2-Chloropyridines with Alkyl Bromides
Reaction: 2-chloropyridines + alkyl bromides → 2-alkylated pyridines

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Allylation of alpha-Amido Sulfones To Form Protected Homoallylic Amines
Reaction: alpha-amido sulfones + allylic acetates → protected homoallylic amines

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Reductive Cross-Coupling of Benzyl Halides with Aryl Halides
Reaction: benzyl chlorides + aryl bromides → diarylmethanes

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Reductive Cross-Coupling of Aryl Triflates and Nonaflates with Alkyl Iodides
Reaction: aryl triflates/nonaflates + alkyl iodides → alkylated arenes

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Reductive Cross-Coupling of Oxalates Derived from alpha-Hydroxy Carbonyls with Vinyl Bromides
Reaction: α-hydroxy carbonyl-derived oxalates (carbonyl radical precursor via Barton C-O scission) + vinyl bromides → beta,γ-unsaturated carbonyl compounds
Ligand Knowledge: The dramatic superiority of imidazole over conventional bidentate ligands is attributed to high loading functioning as both ligand and additive, potentially stabilizing intermediates in the radical chain process.

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Reductive Cross-Coupling of Benzylic Sulfonium Salts with Aryl Iodides
Reaction: benzylic sulfonium triflates + aryl iodides → diarylmethanes

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Reductive Alkylation of Halogenated Pyridines with Secondary Alkyl Bromides
Reaction: halogenated pyridines + secondary alkyl bromides → alkylated pyridines

Title: A Novel Method of Arylation of α-Chloroketones
Reaction: aryl halides (aryl iodides and bromides, variously substituted) + α-chloroketones (chloroacetone, 3-chlorobutanone-2, α-chloroacetophenone) → α-arylketones

Title: Ni-catalyzed deaminative cross-electrophile coupling of Katritzky salts with halides via C-N bond activation
Reaction: aryl iodides + Katritzky pyridinium salts → alkylated arenes; bromoalkynes + Katritzky pyridinium salts → alkynylated products (C(sp)-C(sp3))
Ligand Knowledge: The tridentate terpy ligand with NiBr2(diglyme) afforded the model product in 77% yield, while bidentate ligands dtbbpy gave only trace amounts, demonstrating the additional coordinating site was crucial for aryl iodide reactivity. For C(sp2)-C(sp3) coupling with bromoalkynes, however, bidentate dtbbpy with Ni(acac)2 proved optimal, delivering alkyne products in 52-71% yield.

Title: Modular terpene synthesis enabled by mild electrochemical couplings
Reaction: redox-active esters (NHPI esters from carboxylic acids) + vinyl iodides → sp2-sp3 coupled terpene precursors
Ligand Knowledge: 2,2'-Bpy with NiCl2-6H2O, AgNO3 on Ag-NP-functionalized RVC cathodes provided 50% isolated yield for the model substrate. Extensive screening of ligands, Ni sources, and other metals identified bpy as optimum. The Ag-nanoparticle electrode functionalization was critical. The method was applied to 13 terpene natural product syntheses with broad functional group tolerance and stereoretentive coupling.

Title: A Strained Vicinal Diol as a Reductant for Coupling of Organyl Halides
Reaction: aryl bromides + alkyl iodides → alkylarenes

Title: Nickel-Catalyzed Reductive Methylation of Alkyl Acid with Methyl p-Tosylate
Reaction: alkyl carboxylic acids + methyl p-tosylate → methyl ketones
